nodes.context: Colombian drug cartel founded by Pablo Escobar, monopolized cocaine trade to the US in the 1980s and early 1990s.
Rival Colombian drug cartel that dominated the cocaine market after Escobar's death.
Colombian left-wing guerrilla group hired by Escobar to carry out bombings and assassinations.
Organization founded by Escobar to fight extradition policy, accused of supporting the Palace of Justice siege.
Colombian drug cartel founded by Pablo Escobar, monopolized cocaine trade to the US in the 1980s and early 1990s.
Colombian guerrilla group that carried out the 1985 Palace of Justice siege, financed by Escobar.
Colombian drug cartel, rival of the Medellín Cartel, involved in conflict with Escobar.
Group that helped hunt Escobar, mentioned in the context of his death.
Colombian drug cartel founded by Pablo Escobar, monopolized cocaine trade to the US in the 1980s and early 1990s.
Colombian drug cartel that contracted mercenaries to assassinate Pablo Escobar in 1989.
Rival cartel depicted in the TV series El cartel, whose hitmen kill Escobar.

edges.evidence_quote: He quickly became one of the richest people in the world, but constantly battled rival cartels domestically and abroad
edges.context: The Medellín Cartel battled rival cartels, including the Cali Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Escobar also maintained relations with the Sandinista government in Nicaragua and hired the National Liberation Army (ELN) to carry out bombings and assassinations against government targets.
edges.context: Escobar hired the ELN to carry out bombings and assassinations.

edges.evidence_quote: Escobar subsequently founded and implicitly supported the Los Extraditable Organization, which aims to fight the extradition policy.
edges.context: Escobar founded and supported Los Extraditables to fight extradition.

edges.evidence_quote: She stated that Escobar had financed the operation, which was committed by M-19
edges.context: Escobar financed the 1985 Palace of Justice siege carried out by M-19.

edges.evidence_quote: Members of the Cali Cartel even replayed their recordings of her conversations with Pablo for their wives to demonstrate how a woman should behave.
edges.context: Cali Cartel had recordings of Escobar's wife and demanded reparations for Escobar's war against them.

edges.evidence_quote: The building was initially built for Escobar's wife but was gutted by a Cali Cartel car bomb in 1988
edges.context: Cali Cartel bombed Escobar's apartment building in 1988.

edges.evidence_quote: Killing Escobar was a documentary televised in the UK in 2021. It concerned a failed attempt by mercenaries, contracted by the Cali Cartel and led by Peter McAleese, to assassinate Escobar in 1989.
edges.context: The Cali Cartel contracted mercenaries to assassinate Pablo Escobar, leader of the Medellín Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Caracol TV produced a television series, El cartel (The Cartel), which began airing on 4 June 2008 where Escobar is portrayed by an unknown model when he is shot down by Cartel del Sur's hitmen.
edges.context: In the TV series El cartel, Escobar is killed by hitmen from Cartel del Sur, indicating a conflict between the Medellín Cartel and Cartel del Sur.
